verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, which consists of sperm-oocyte fusion proteins canonically expressed in the germline. NCBI lists it as nuclear-resident, but IZUMO-family proteins are known to traffic to the oolemma (outer leaflet of the egg plasma membrane) during oogenesis and to be involved in sperm-egg fusion — a documented germline / reproductive-lineage-restricted surface display. The protein has a predicted TM domain consistent with IZUMO-family architecture. Surface display in this context is tissue-restricted (germline lineages only), meeting the definition of `contextual / tissue_restricted_surface`.
predicted_key_uncertainty: NCBI summary calls the protein nuclear-resident, and no direct cell-surface biotinylation or surface-proteomics data for IZUMO4 itself appear in public databases; confidence rests on IZUMO-family-lineage biology and documented oocyte-membrane localization of IZUMO-family members (especially IZUMO1). Direct experimental confirmation of IZUMO4 surface display on oocytes would upgrade confidence to high.
